text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
One day, while I'm at the library trying<n>to come
up with a training plan...

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text: No, that's too safe as well...

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Indeed. I have a tendency to take the<n>safe route
no matter what I do,<n>whether it be in races or
training.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
When it rains, I do the same amount<n>of exercise
indoors. When I am<n>fatigued, I readjust my
schedule so I<n>can rest.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Establishing such safe parameters<n>makes for
predictable results. On the<n>other hand, taking
risks invites<n>unpredictability, which can be a<n>
curse... but also a boon.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
As such, I have concluded that there<n>is value in
taking some risks in order<n>to expand my line of
reasoning.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
I forced myself out of my comfort<n>zone and
began planning a workout<n>on a dirt course to
avoid injury...

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
But that's when I realized I was<n>taking the safe
option once again!
choice_data_list: Don't think with your head—just act.
It'll only do you harm if you injure yourself.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text: My head? Are you trying to imply that<n>it's—oh.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Every time someone mentions<n>something about
my head, well, it<n>makes me think they're teasing
me<n>about how big it is...

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
My apologies. You were trying to tell<n>me that
theories are just empty<n>words unless I act on
them, correct?

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Act rather than think... I suppose that<n>is the more
reckless path.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text: Thank you. I think I've had a small<n>epiphany.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Are you saying that erring on the side<n>of caution
is acceptable? I suppose I<n>should take a
trainer's word for it.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Hm... Then that means I can still use<n>this thesis I
wrote up.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Oh! Do you think you could read<n>through it? If you
don't mind, that is.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text: I call it "Safe Training with Reasonable<n>Risk."

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Biwa Hayahide shows me her paper,<n>and her
methods are indeed cautious...<n>but they should
make for safe and<n>effective training.
